title: What Has Changed in My Relationship With Money?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for marking the shift in your financial relationship and anchoring it as permanent
excerpt: A five-prompt journal set from Day 70 of a 12-week money program. The prompts move from taking stock of how much has changed across the work to explicitly marking the new relationship with money as a permanent one. A completion-point journal set.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 70 theme of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

content.text: A five-prompt journal set from Day 70 of a 12-week money program. Day 70 is a completion point: Week 10 closes with an invitation to mark how much has changed and to anchor that change as a permanent new baseline rather than a good patch. Set a timer. Write without editing.
New to journal prompts as a practice? Read
What was my relationship with money like before this work began?
Write the specifics: the feelings, the habits, the recurring thoughts.
What is my relationship with money like now?
Write what is actually different, not what you hope is different.
What specific moment or turning point stands out from the practice work as the one where something clearly shifted?
Write the moment in as much detail as you remember.
What do I need to keep doing to hold this new relationship with money?
Write the specific practices, habits, or patterns, not a general intention.
Complete this sentence in three different ways: 'The new version of me with money...'
Write three different completions. The one that makes you emotional is worth keeping.
Adapted from the Day 70 theme of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
